findings.evidence: Lines 136-146 define `toBoundedFun`, `toBoundedFun_injective`, then use them immediately in `Fintype.ofInjective`; grep finds no other use.
findings.fix: Make both declarations `private` or move them into a private section, leaving the `Fintype (DegreeLE ι k)` instance and `finite_setOf_order_le` as the public finiteness API.
findings.issue: `toBoundedFun` and `toBoundedFun_injective` are public, but they are only the encoding used to construct `degreeLEFintype`, not part of the Sobolev-facing multiindex API.

findings.evidence: Lines 52-54 prove `order (α + β) = order α + order β`; lines 110-112 prove `unit i j = 0` under `j ≠ i`; nearby `order_zero`, `order_single`, `order_unit`, and `unit_apply_self` are already `[simp]`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `order_add` and `unit_apply_ne` if they remain public; both rewrite to smaller normal forms and should not loop.
findings.issue: The characteristic normal forms `order_add` and `unit_apply_ne` are not tagged `[simp]`, so users cannot normalize `order (α + β)` or off-coordinate `unit i j` without naming these lemmas.

summary: The API exposes an implementation encoding for the finiteness proof and leaves basic normal forms out of `simp`, so downstream Sobolev code will either depend on internals or need avoidable manual rewrites.
